title: prunella vulgaris subsp. laciniata
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. laciniata, commonly known as self-heal or prunella, is a subspecies of the Prunella vulgaris plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. laciniata include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activities, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Prunella vulgaris subsp. laciniata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
